_purpose: LLM-readable mirror of the GOVERNANCE.md routing table. Lets agents (Codex, MCP servers, future bots) answer 'where does this kind of rule live?' without parsing markdown. Treat GOVERNANCE.md as the human-readable source of truth; this file is the machine-readable mirror. If they disagree, the markdown wins and this file is stale.

rule_types.name: session_behavior
rule_types.description: Behavior of every coding-agent session (Hard Rules, session protocols, Karpathy principles, Codex-First).

rule_types.name: folder_purpose_and_structure
rule_types.description: Folder purpose + structure: every top-level folder needs an AGENTS.md or README.md.
rule_types.review_cadence: Monthly sandbox sweep; per-PR via hook

rule_types.name: cross_session_knowledge
rule_types.description: Cross-session knowledge (case law). Memory entries with frontmatter.
enforcement: Frontmatter discipline (scripts/check_memory_frontmatter.py for in-repo mirror at docs/memory/)

rule_types.name: multi_session_projects
rule_types.description: Multi-session projects: atlas, harvest, studio, echo, dashboards4sale, compass.

rule_types.name: code_level_invariants
rule_types.description: Code-level invariants: em-dashes, secrets, folder-purpose, memory-frontmatter, doc-size, redundancy, session-log.

rule_types.name: local_llm_context_per_folder
rule_types.description: Local LLM context per folder: AGENTS.md (or README.md for non-rules folders like sandbox/).

rule_types.name: platform_vs_satellite_boundary
rule_types.description: Platform vs satellite boundary: when a venture has its own URL/customer/revenue it becomes a satellite repo.
enforcement: Manual review when a venture has its own URL/customer/revenue

conflict_resolution.description: Boubacar's explicit instruction in the current conversation overrides every doc and hook.
If a pre-commit hook fails, the commit fails, regardless of what any markdown file says. Mechanism wins.
If AGENT_SOP.md and a memory file contradict, AGENT_SOP wins. Memory is case law; AGENT_SOP is constitutional.
Local context can extend or specialize, but cannot contradict AGENT_SOP.

retirement_protocol.principle: Every rule that exists must justify itself today, not three years ago.

quarterly_purge.method: git log --since='90 days ago' against each governance surface; any rule untouched 90+ days AND not referenced by any commit, hook, or memory entry is a candidate for retirement.

manifest_must_include: reference-grep result that confirmed zero callers

resurrection.method: Add the rule back to its source surface and cite the manifest entry that explains the prior retirement.

steps: Decide which surface owns the rule (use the rule_types routing above).
Write it in that surface, with a *Why:* line citing the incident or decision that motivated it.
If the rule is enforceable by mechanism, add a hook in the same commit. Documents that govern without mechanism decay in 14 days.
If the rule conflicts with an existing rule, retire the older one or reconcile explicitly. Two contradictory rules in two surfaces is the sprawl pattern.

pre_commit.scope: rule corpus (skill-payload AGENTS.md exempt; roadmaps exempt)

_validation.drift_check: Run validator after editing GOVERNANCE.md or this manifest. Fails when the markdown's routing table or rule_types are out of sync with this JSON.
